description: Create a content slide with two-column layout on future regulatory priorities, featuring two horizontal bar charts: one for top impacts on risk management and one for risk areas expected to see greater supervisory focus.

header.sub: Looking out over the next three years, more scrutiny of tech and AI-related risk (cited by 63% of CROs) and the need for increased investment in risk data and analytics capabilities to meet supervisory expectations (51%) top the list of expected impacts of an evolving regulatory landscape. Future regulatory priorities: CROs expect cyber and technology risk (cited by 71% of our survey respondents) to see the greatest increase in supervisory focus, followed by digital fraud (36%), financial crime (36%) and third-party risk (35%). ESG will see less supervisory focus in the future, according to 55% of respondents. Strategic risk (46%) and conduct risk (35%) were also expected to fall as supervisory priorities.

left.title: Figure 18: Top three impacts on risk management from evolving global regulation during the next three years

series.label: Elevated scrutiny of technology and AI-related risk due to rising regulatory attention
Greater investment in risk data and analytics capabilities to meet supervisory expectations
Enhanced third-party and operational resilience measures in response to fragmented regimes
Strengthened governance and control frameworks to align with emerging standards
Enhanced focus on internal risk management robustness and quality
Increased emphasis on operational efficiency while maintaining risk discipline

labels: Elevated scrutiny of technology and AI-related risk due to rising regulatory attention
Greater investment in risk data and analytics capabilities to meet supervisory expectations
Enhanced third-party and operational resilience measures in response to fragmented regimes
Strengthened governance and control frameworks to align with emerging standards

Increased emphasis on operational efficiency while maintaining risk discipline

right.title: Figure 19: Risk areas that will see the greatest increase in supervisory focus in the next three years

series.label: Financial crime risk (e.g., KYC, AML, sanctions, bribery)
Digital fraud risk (e.g., scams, phishing, targeting clients)
